title: OAuth, Secrets, Identity, and Least Privilege · MCP Build Room
summary: Secure a remote MCP server serving user-specific data. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an OAuth and credential-handling design while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decide when authentication and user authorization are required by data and action scope.
Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Use a changed workshop case for agent ext authorization that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
